questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-24, cash flows from discontinued operations may be presented separately within each cash flow category (operating, investing, and financing) on the face of the statement, or the information may be disclosed in the notes. There is no requirement to create a separate fourth section, nor to exclude them. The entity has flexibility in how to present this information. The correct answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-24に基づき、非継続事業からのCFは、CF計算書の各区分（営業、投資、財務）内で区分表示するか、注記で開示することができる。第4のセクションを作成する要件も、除外する要件もない。表示方法には柔軟性がある。正解はA。

questions.stem: Entity HH collected $120,000 on a note receivable from a customer. The original $100,000 loan was made two years ago, and $20,000 represents interest. How are these amounts classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Principal of $100,000 as investing inflow; interest of $20,000 as operating inflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: Principal of $100,000 as operating inflow; interest of $20,000 as investing inflow

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-12, the collection of a loan (principal of $100,000) is classified as an investing inflow — it is the return of an investing activity. Under ASC 230-10-45-16, the interest collected ($20,000) is classified as an operating inflow under U.S. GAAP. The two components are reported in different sections of the cash flow statement. The correct answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-12に基づき、貸付金の回収（元本$100,000）は投資活動の流入に分類される。ASC 230-10-45-16に基づき、回収した利息（$20,000）はUS GAAPでは営業活動の流入に分類される。2つの構成要素はCF計算書の異なるセクションで報告される。正解はA。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Deferred Tax Liability Change
questions.stem: Marlow Inc. reported net income of $620,000. During the year its deferred tax liability increased by $34,000 and its deferred tax asset increased by $11,000. Depreciation was $72,000. Assuming no other adjustments, what is cash provided by operating activities under the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Start with net income $620,000. Add back depreciation $72,000 (non-cash). An increase in a deferred tax liability means recorded tax expense exceeded taxes actually paid, so add $34,000. An increase in a deferred tax asset means recorded tax expense was less than taxes paid (cash outflow not yet expensed), so subtract $11,000. Operating cash flow = $620,000 + $72,000 + $34,000 - $11,000 = $715,000.
questions.explanationJa: 純利益$620,000から出発する。減価償却費$72,000を加算（非現金）。繰延税金負債の増加は計上税金費用が実際の納税額を上回ったことを意味するため$34,000を加算する。繰延税金資産の増加は計上税金費用が納税額より少なかったことを意味するため$11,000を減算する。営業CF = $620,000 + $72,000 + $34,000 - $11,000 = $715,000。

questions.stem: Quill Corp. issued bonds at a premium. During the year, amortization of the bond premium was $9,000, depreciation was $55,000, and net income was $410,000. Accounts receivable decreased by $18,000. There were no other adjustments. What is cash provided by operating activities under the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Net income $410,000 + depreciation $55,000 (non-cash) - bond premium amortization $9,000 + decrease in AR $18,000 = $474,000. Bond premium amortization reduces interest expense without a cash effect, so the amount that lowered interest expense (and raised net income) must be subtracted. A decrease in accounts receivable is added because cash collected exceeded revenue recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 純利益$410,000 + 減価償却費$55,000（非現金）- 社債プレミアム償却$9,000 + 売掛金減少$18,000 = $474,000。社債プレミアム償却は現金影響なしに利息費用を減少させるため、純利益を増加させた分を減算する。売掛金の減少は回収額が収益を上回ったため加算する。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Paid for Operating Expenses
questions.stem: Tate Co. reported operating expenses (excluding depreciation) of $480,000. During the year, prepaid expenses increased by $14,000 and accrued liabilities increased by $9,000. Under the direct method, what is cash paid for operating expenses?
questions.explanation: Cash paid for operating expenses = Operating expenses + Increase in prepaid expenses - Increase in accrued liabilities. An increase in prepaid expenses means cash was paid in advance beyond the expense recognized (add $14,000). An increase in accrued liabilities means expenses were recognized but not yet paid (subtract $9,000). Cash paid = $480,000 + $14,000 - $9,000 = $485,000.
questions.explanationJa: 営業費用の現金支払額 = 営業費用 + 前払費用の増加 - 未払負債の増加。前払費用の増加は認識費用を超えて前払いしたことを意味する（$14,000加算）。未払負債の増加は費用を認識したが未払いであることを意味する（$9,000減算）。現金支払額 = $480,000 + $14,000 - $9,000 = $485,000。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Paid for Income Taxes
questions.stem: Brimm Corp. reported income tax expense of $185,000. During the year, income taxes payable decreased by $12,000 and the deferred tax liability increased by $20,000. Under the direct method, what is cash paid for income taxes?
questions.explanation: Cash paid for income taxes = Income tax expense + Decrease in income taxes payable - Increase in deferred tax liability. A decrease in income taxes payable means more cash was paid than the current expense (add $12,000). An increase in the deferred tax liability is a non-cash portion of tax expense (subtract $20,000). Cash paid = $185,000 + $12,000 - $20,000 = $177,000.
questions.explanationJa: 法人税の現金支払額 = 法人税費用 + 未払法人税の減少 - 繰延税金負債の増加。未払法人税の減少は当期費用を超えて現金を支払ったことを意味する（$12,000加算）。繰延税金負債の増加は税金費用の非現金部分である（$20,000減算）。現金支払額 = $185,000 + $12,000 - $20,000 = $177,000。

questions.stem: Vance Inc. reported cash provided by operating activities of $540,000. Adjustments included: depreciation $80,000; gain on sale of equipment $25,000; increase in accounts receivable $40,000; decrease in inventory $15,000; increase in accounts payable $30,000. Working backward, what was net income?
questions.explanation: Operating cash flow = Net income + depreciation - gain - increase in AR + decrease in inventory + increase in AP. Solve for net income: $540,000 = NI + $80,000 - $25,000 - $40,000 + $15,000 + $30,000. The adjustments net to +$60,000, so NI = $540,000 - $60,000 = $480,000. Verify: $480,000 + $80,000 - $25,000 - $40,000 + $15,000 + $30,000 = $540,000.

questions.subtopic: Investing - Net PP&E Purchases from Rollforward
questions.stem: Holt Corp.'s gross property, plant, and equipment increased from $1,400,000 to $1,650,000 during the year. During the year the company sold equipment that had originally cost $90,000 (no equipment was acquired through noncash means). What amount of cash was used to purchase PP&E?
questions.explanation: Gross PP&E rollforward: Beginning + Purchases - Cost of assets disposed = Ending. $1,400,000 + Purchases - $90,000 = $1,650,000. Solve: Purchases = $1,650,000 - $1,400,000 + $90,000 = $340,000. The disposal removed $90,000 of original cost, so total purchases must have exceeded the net increase by that amount. The cash investing outflow for PP&E purchases is $340,000.
questions.explanationJa: 有形固定資産（取得原価）のロールフォワード：期首 + 取得 - 処分資産の取得原価 = 期末。$1,400,000 + 取得 - $90,000 = $1,650,000。取得 = $1,650,000 - $1,400,000 + $90,000 = $340,000。処分により取得原価$90,000が除却されたため、総取得額は純増加額をその分上回る。PP&E取得の投資活動現金流出は$340,000。

questions.subtopic: Financing - Note Issued and Repaid During Year
questions.stem: During the year, Ames Co. borrowed $500,000 under a short-term note and later repaid $300,000 of that note. It also issued common stock for $220,000 and repurchased treasury stock for $90,000. No dividends were paid. Under U.S. GAAP, what is net cash provided by financing activities?
questions.explanation: Each financing item is reported at its gross cash amount. Borrowing: +$500,000. Repayment: -$300,000. Stock issuance: +$220,000. Treasury stock repurchase: -$90,000. Net financing = $500,000 - $300,000 + $220,000 - $90,000 = $330,000. Borrowings and repayments of debt are generally reported gross, not netted, except for certain short-maturity items.
questions.explanationJa: 各財務項目は総額の現金額で報告する。借入：+$500,000。返済：-$300,000。株式発行：+$220,000。自己株式取得：-$90,000。財務活動純額 = $500,000 - $300,000 + $220,000 - $90,000 = $330,000。借入と返済は一部の短期項目を除き原則として総額表示する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 230-10-45-7 through 45-9, which of the following items may be reported on a net basis (rather than gross) on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cash receipts and payments for items with quick turnover, large amounts, and short maturities (e.g., demand deposits with related principal amounts due in three months or less)

choices.label: B
choices.text: All financing activities for the period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Proceeds from issuing long-term bonds and repayment of those bonds

choices.label: D
choices.text: Purchases and sales of property, plant, and equipment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-8 and 45-9, net reporting is permitted for items where turnover is quick, the amounts are large, and the maturities are short (generally three months or less)—for example, certain investments, loans receivable, and debt. Long-term debt issuances and repayments, PP&E transactions, and financing activities generally must be reported gross. The correct answer describes the qualifying conditions for net reporting.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-8および45-9により、回転が速く、金額が大きく、満期が短い（原則3ヶ月以内）項目については純額表示が認められる（例：特定の投資、貸付金、債務）。長期債務の発行・返済、PP&E取引、財務活動は原則として総額表示が必要。正解は純額表示の適格要件を述べている。

questions.stem: Korbin Inc. recorded a goodwill impairment loss of $130,000 and an inventory write-down to net realizable value of $25,000 during the year. Net income was $300,000 and depreciation was $48,000. No other adjustments apply. What is cash provided by operating activities under the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Net income $300,000 + depreciation $48,000 + goodwill impairment $130,000 + inventory write-down $25,000 = $503,000. The goodwill impairment loss is a non-cash charge added back. The inventory write-down reduced net income but used no cash (it lowered the carrying amount of inventory without a cash payment), so it is also added back as a non-cash item.
questions.explanationJa: 純利益$300,000 + 減価償却費$48,000 + のれん減損$130,000 + 棚卸資産評価減$25,000 = $503,000。のれん減損損失は非現金費用のため加算する。棚卸資産評価減は純利益を減少させたが現金を使用していない（現金支払なしに棚卸資産の帳簿価額を引き下げた）ため、非現金項目として加算する。

questions.stem: Delaney Corp. recognized net periodic pension cost of $95,000 in the income statement but contributed $130,000 in cash to the pension plan trust during the year. Using the indirect method, what is the net adjustment related to the pension in the operating section?
questions.explanation: Pension expense of $95,000 reduced net income, but the actual cash contribution was $130,000. Because the cash outflow ($130,000) exceeded the expense recognized ($95,000) by $35,000, an additional $35,000 must be subtracted from net income to reflect the higher cash usage. This is equivalent to recognizing that the prepaid pension asset increased (or accrued pension liability decreased) by $35,000.
questions.explanationJa: 年金費用$95,000は純利益を減少させたが、実際の現金拠出は$130,000であった。現金流出$130,000が認識費用$95,000を$35,000上回るため、追加で$35,000を純利益から減算してより多くの現金使用を反映する。これは前払年金資産が$35,000増加（または未払年金負債が$35,000減少）したことを認識することと同じである。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-15 (ASC 230-10-45-15), when a debtor settles a zero-coupon bond (or a bond with an insignificant coupon relative to the effective interest rate) at maturity, how is the cash payment classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The portion attributable to accreted interest is operating; the portion attributable to the original principal is financing

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entire payment is classified as financing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entire payment is classified as operating activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entire payment is classified as investing activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-15 (as amended by ASU 2016-15), at settlement of a zero-coupon debt instrument, the portion of the cash payment attributable to accreted interest is classified as an operating activity, and the portion attributable to the original proceeds (principal) is classified as a financing activity. This splits the lump-sum maturity payment between the interest and financing components.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-15（ASU 2016-15により改正）により、ゼロクーポン債務の決済時には、現金支払のうち発生利息（アクリーション）に帰属する部分は営業活動に、当初収入（元本）に帰属する部分は財務活動に分類する。これにより満期一括払いを利息部分と財務部分に分ける。

questions.stem: Renner Corp. extinguished a long-term note before maturity. It paid $1,000,000 of principal plus a $40,000 prepayment penalty. Under ASU 2016-15, how is the prepayment penalty classified on the statement of cash flows?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-15 (as clarified by ASU 2016-15), cash payments for debt prepayment or debt extinguishment costs—including prepayment penalties and third-party costs—are classified as financing activities. The $40,000 penalty is reported as a financing outflow along with the $1,000,000 principal repayment. This resolved prior diversity in practice.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-15（ASU 2016-15により明確化）により、債務の早期返済または消滅に係る現金支払（早期返済違約金や第三者費用を含む）は財務活動に分類する。$40,000の違約金は$1,000,000の元本返済とともに財務活動の流出として報告する。これは従来の実務上の多様性を解消した。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Proceeds from Corporate-Owned Life Insurance
questions.stem: Sutter Inc. received $600,000 in death-benefit proceeds from a corporate-owned life insurance (COLI) policy. Under ASU 2016-15, how are proceeds from the settlement of corporate-owned life insurance policies classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operating or investing, at the entity's election

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21B (added by ASU 2016-15), cash proceeds received from the settlement of corporate-owned life insurance policies, including bank-owned life insurance (BOLI) policies, are classified as cash inflows from investing activities. The related premium payments may be classified as investing, operating, or a combination. The $600,000 death benefit is an investing inflow.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21B（ASU 2016-15により追加）により、法人保有生命保険（COLI、銀行保有生命保険BOLIを含む）の決済による現金収入は投資活動からの流入に分類する。関連する保険料支払は投資、営業、またはその組合せで分類できる。$600,000の死亡給付金は投資活動の流入である。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-15, when a transferor receives a beneficial interest in exchange for transferring trade receivables in a securitization, how are subsequent cash receipts from that beneficial interest classified on the statement of cash flows?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21B (per ASU 2016-15), cash receipts from payments on a transferor's beneficial interests obtained in a securitization of financial assets are classified as investing activities. (The initial transfer of the receivables in exchange for the beneficial interest is itself a noncash activity that must be disclosed.) The subsequent collections on the retained beneficial interest are investing inflows.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21B（ASU 2016-15）により、金融資産の証券化で譲渡人が取得した受益持分に対する支払からの現金収入は投資活動に分類する。（受益持分と引き換えに行う売掛金の当初譲渡自体は開示すべき非現金活動である。）保有する受益持分に対するその後の回収は投資活動の流入である。

questions.subtopic: Investing - Proceeds from Sale of Equipment Calculation
questions.stem: Fenwick Co. sold equipment that had an original cost of $260,000 and accumulated depreciation of $190,000. The company recorded a loss on the sale of $12,000. What amount of cash proceeds is reported as an investing inflow?
questions.explanation: Book value = Cost - Accumulated depreciation = $260,000 - $190,000 = $70,000. Loss on sale = Book value - Cash proceeds, so $12,000 = $70,000 - Proceeds. Proceeds = $70,000 - $12,000 = $58,000. The full $58,000 cash received is reported as an investing inflow. (The $12,000 loss is added back to net income in the operating section under the indirect method.)
questions.explanationJa: 簿価 = 取得原価 - 減価償却累計額 = $260,000 - $190,000 = $70,000。売却損 = 簿価 - 現金収入なので$12,000 = $70,000 - 収入。収入 = $70,000 - $12,000 = $58,000。受取現金$58,000全額が投資活動の流入として報告される。（$12,000の売却損は間接法で営業活動の純利益に加算される。）

questions.subtopic: Operating - Cash Generated from Operations Total
questions.stem: Galway Inc. reports: net income $700,000; depreciation $90,000; amortization $15,000; deferred income tax expense $22,000; loss on early extinguishment of debt $18,000; increase in accounts receivable $55,000; increase in inventory $40,000; increase in accounts payable $33,000; decrease in accrued liabilities $12,000. What is net cash provided by operating activities?
questions.explanation: Net income $700,000 + depreciation $90,000 + amortization $15,000 + deferred tax expense $22,000 + loss on extinguishment $18,000 - increase in AR $55,000 - increase in inventory $40,000 + increase in AP $33,000 - decrease in accrued liabilities $12,000 = $771,000. Non-cash charges and the loss are added; the loss on extinguishment is non-operating but the related cash settlement is financing, so the loss is added back here. Working capital: asset increases subtract, liability increases add, liability decreases subtract.
questions.explanationJa: 純利益$700,000 + 減価償却費$90,000 + 償却$15,000 + 繰延税金費用$22,000 + 債務消滅損$18,000 - 売掛金増加$55,000 - 棚卸資産増加$40,000 + 買掛金増加$33,000 - 未払負債減少$12,000 = $771,000。非現金費用と損失は加算。債務消滅損は営業外だが関連する現金決済は財務活動のため、損失をここで加算する。運転資本：資産増加は減算、負債増加は加算、負債減少は減算。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Distribution from Equity Investee (Nature Approach)
questions.stem: Pearce Inc. uses the nature of the distribution approach for distributions received from equity method investees. It received a $90,000 distribution that the investee designated as a return generated by the investee's normal operating activities. How should Pearce classify this distribution?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Split equally between operating and investing

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21D (ASU 2016-15), the nature of the distribution approach classifies each distribution based on the nature of the activity that generated it. A distribution arising from the investee's normal operating activities (a return on investment) is classified as an operating cash inflow. A distribution that is a return of investment (e.g., from a sale of assets by the investee) would be investing. Here, the $90,000 is operating.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21D（ASU 2016-15）により、分配の性質アプローチでは各分配をそれを生み出した活動の性質に基づき分類する。投資先の通常の営業活動から生じた分配（投資収益）は営業活動の流入に分類する。投資の回収である分配（投資先の資産売却など）は投資活動となる。本問の$90,000は営業活動である。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Allowance for Credit Losses
questions.stem: Stone Corp. reported net income of $440,000. Bad debt (credit loss) expense for the year was $28,000, of which $20,000 of specific accounts were written off against the allowance. Gross accounts receivable increased by $35,000 during the year. Depreciation was $60,000. What is cash provided by operating activities under the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Convert the change in gross accounts receivable to the change in net receivables. The allowance for credit losses rolls forward as: bad debt expense $28,000 - write-offs $20,000 = a $8,000 increase. Net AR change = increase in gross AR $35,000 - increase in allowance $8,000 = $27,000 net increase. Under the indirect method, the bad debt expense and write-offs net into the allowance and are already captured in the net receivable change, so they are not added separately. Operating cash flow = net income $440,000 + depreciation $60,000 - increase in net AR $27,000 = $473,000.
questions.explanationJa: 総売掛金の変動を純売掛金の変動に変換する。貸倒引当金のロールフォワード：貸倒費用$28,000 - 貸倒償却$20,000 = 引当金$8,000増加。純売掛金の変動 = 総売掛金増加$35,000 - 引当金増加$8,000 = 純額$27,000増加。間接法では、貸倒費用と貸倒償却は引当金に相殺され純売掛金の変動に既に反映されているため、別途加算しない。営業CF = 純利益$440,000 + 減価償却費$60,000 - 純売掛金増加$27,000 = $473,000。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Cash Surrender Value Premium Split
questions.stem: Aldridge Corp. pays a $50,000 annual premium on a corporate-owned life insurance policy. Of the premium, $30,000 increases the policy's cash surrender value and $20,000 is a mortality/cost charge. Under ASU 2016-15, how may the entity classify the premium payment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Investing, operating, or a combination, applied consistently

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entirely as operating activities, with no alternative

choices.label: D
choices.text: Entirely as investing activities, with no alternative

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21B (ASU 2016-15), while proceeds from settlement of COLI policies are investing inflows, premium payments may be classified as cash outflows from investing activities, operating activities, or a combination of the two. The entity selects a policy and applies it consistently. Thus the $50,000 premium classification is a policy election among those alternatives.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21B（ASU 2016-15）により、COLI決済による収入は投資活動の流入だが、保険料支払は投資活動、営業活動、またはその組合せのいずれかに分類できる。事業体は方針を選択し一貫して適用する。したがって$50,000の保険料の分類はこれらの選択肢の中での会計方針の選択である。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Received from Customers with Advances
questions.stem: Whitlock Co. reports sales revenue of $1,200,000. During the year, accounts receivable increased by $45,000 and unearned (deferred) revenue increased by $30,000. Under the direct method, what is cash received from customers?
questions.explanation: Cash received from customers = Sales revenue - Increase in accounts receivable + Increase in unearned revenue. The increase in AR means some recognized sales were not yet collected (subtract $45,000). The increase in unearned revenue means cash was collected in advance of recognizing revenue (add $30,000). Cash received = $1,200,000 - $45,000 + $30,000 = $1,185,000.
questions.explanationJa: 顧客からの現金受取額 = 売上高 - 売掛金の増加 + 前受収益の増加。売掛金の増加は認識した売上の一部が未回収であることを意味する（$45,000減算）。前受収益の増加は収益認識前に現金を回収したことを意味する（$30,000加算）。現金受取額 = $1,200,000 - $45,000 + $30,000 = $1,185,000。

questions.stem: Bexley Inc. had the following: net cash from operating activities $410,000; net cash used in investing activities $(260,000); net cash from financing activities $90,000; effect of exchange rate changes on cash $(15,000). Beginning cash and cash equivalents were $180,000. What is the ending balance of cash and cash equivalents?
questions.explanation: Net change in cash = Operating + Investing + Financing + Effect of exchange rate changes = $410,000 - $260,000 + $90,000 - $15,000 = $225,000. Ending cash = Beginning cash + Net change = $180,000 + $225,000 = $405,000. The exchange rate effect is included as a separate reconciling item, not within any of the three activity sections.
questions.explanationJa: 現金の純変動 = 営業 + 投資 + 財務 + 為替レート変動の影響 = $410,000 - $260,000 + $90,000 - $15,000 = $225,000。期末現金 = 期首現金 + 純変動 = $180,000 + $225,000 = $405,000。為替レート変動の影響は3つの活動区分内ではなく独立した調整項目として含める。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Proceeds from Sale of Trading Securities
questions.stem: Crestline Corp. is a manufacturer (not a broker-dealer) that holds trading debt securities. During the year it sold trading securities for $340,000 cash. Under ASU 2016-15, how are cash flows from the purchase and sale of trading debt securities classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Based on the nature and purpose for which the securities were acquired

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-19 (clarified by ASU 2016-15), cash receipts and payments related to securities classified as trading (debt securities under ASC 320 / equity securities under ASC 321 held for trading) are classified based on the nature and purpose for which the securities were acquired. If acquired specifically for trading, they are operating; otherwise classification follows their purpose. There is no single mandatory category.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-19（ASU 2016-15により明確化）により、売買目的に分類される有価証券（ASC 320の負債証券、ASC 321の売買目的の持分証券）に係る現金収支は、その取得の性質と目的に基づき分類する。売買目的で取得した場合は営業活動、それ以外は目的に従う。単一の強制区分はない。

questions.subtopic: Operating - Amortization of Right-of-Use Asset (Operating Lease)
questions.stem: Under the indirect method, how is the amortization (single straight-line lease cost recognition) of an operating lease right-of-use asset treated in reconciling net income to operating cash flow?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The non-cash portion of operating lease cost (amortization of the ROU asset and accretion of the lease liability) is added back, and the change in the operating lease liability is reflected, so total operating lease cash payments end up in operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is reported as a financing outflow

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is reported as an investing outflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment is ever required because operating leases never affect cash flow

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842, operating lease cost is a single straight-line expense in the income statement. Under the indirect method, the non-cash components (amortization of the right-of-use asset and accretion of the lease liability that make up the expense) are adjusted, and the change in the operating lease liability is included, so that the actual cash paid for operating lease payments is classified entirely within operating activities (ASC 842-20-45-5). Operating lease payments are operating cash flows.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842では、オペレーティング・リース費用は損益計算書上で定額の単一費用として計上される。間接法では、その費用を構成する非現金部分（使用権資産の償却とリース負債のアクリーション）を調整し、オペレーティング・リース負債の変動を含めることで、実際に支払ったオペレーティング・リース料の全額が営業活動内に分類される（ASC 842-20-45-5）。オペレーティング・リース料は営業CFである。

questions.stem: On the lease commencement date, Hadley Co. recognized a right-of-use asset of $850,000 and a corresponding lease liability of $850,000 for a new finance lease. No cash was paid at commencement. How is the initial recognition reported on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Disclosed as a significant noncash investing and financing activity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not reported anywhere because leases are off-balance-sheet

questions.explanation: The initial recognition of a right-of-use asset and lease liability at commencement involves no cash exchange. Under ASC 230-10-50-3 and ASC 842-20-50-4, obtaining a right-of-use asset in exchange for a lease liability is a noncash transaction that must be disclosed as supplemental noncash investing and financing activity. Cash flows occur only as lease payments are made over the term.
questions.explanationJa: リース開始日における使用権資産とリース負債の当初認識は現金の授受を伴わない。ASC 230-10-50-3およびASC 842-20-50-4により、リース負債と引き換えに使用権資産を取得することは非現金取引であり、補足的な非現金の投資・財務活動として開示しなければならない。現金の動きはリース期間にわたるリース料の支払時にのみ生じる。

questions.subtopic: Investing - Net Cash from Investing Section
questions.stem: Larkspur Inc. had the following during the year: purchased equipment for $310,000 cash; sold land for $140,000 cash; purchased held-to-maturity bonds (5-year) for $200,000 cash; collected $60,000 of principal on a long-term note receivable; received $18,000 of interest on the note. What is net cash used in investing activities?
questions.explanation: Investing items only: equipment purchase -$310,000; land sale +$140,000; HTM bond purchase -$200,000; note principal collection +$60,000. The $18,000 interest received is an operating inflow under U.S. GAAP, not investing. Net investing = -$310,000 + $140,000 - $200,000 + $60,000 = -$310,000. Net cash used in investing activities is $310,000.
questions.explanationJa: 投資項目のみ：設備購入-$310,000、土地売却+$140,000、満期保有債券購入-$200,000、手形元本回収+$60,000。受取利息$18,000はUS GAAPでは営業活動の流入であり投資活動ではない。投資活動純額 = -$310,000 + $140,000 - $200,000 + $60,000 = -$310,000。投資活動による現金使用額は$310,000。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Premium Paid on Settlement of Debt at Reacquisition
questions.stem: Trenton Corp. reacquired its outstanding bonds (carrying value $980,000) in the open market for $1,025,000 cash, recognizing a $45,000 loss on extinguishment. How is the total $1,025,000 cash payment classified on the statement of cash flows?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-15, repayment of amounts borrowed (including reacquisition of an entity's own debt) is a financing activity. The entire cash paid to extinguish the debt, $1,025,000, is reported as a financing outflow. The $45,000 loss is a non-cash income statement item that is added back to net income in the operating section under the indirect method; it is not separately classified as operating cash.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-15により、借入金の返済（自社債務の買戻しを含む）は財務活動である。債務消滅のために支払った現金全額$1,025,000を財務活動の流出として報告する。$45,000の損失は非現金の損益項目であり、間接法では営業活動の純利益に加算される。営業活動の現金として別途分類されるものではない。

questions.subtopic: Operating - Dividends Received from Investment (Cumulative Earnings)
questions.stem: Marsh Inc. uses the cumulative earnings approach for an equity method investee. Since acquisition, cumulative equity earnings recognized total $300,000 and cumulative distributions received (before this year) total $240,000. This year Marsh receives a $90,000 distribution. How is the $90,000 classified?
questions.explanation: Under the cumulative earnings approach (ASC 230-10-45-21D), distributions are operating up to cumulative equity earnings; any excess is a return of investment (investing). Cumulative distributions after this year = $240,000 + $90,000 = $330,000. Cumulative earnings = $300,000. The portion of this year's distribution that keeps cumulative distributions at or below $300,000 is operating: $300,000 - $240,000 = $60,000 operating. The excess, $330,000 - $300,000 = $30,000, is investing. Answer: $60,000 operating and $30,000 investing.
questions.explanationJa: 累積利益アプローチ（ASC 230-10-45-21D）では、分配は累積持分利益までは営業活動、超過分は投資の回収（投資活動）となる。本年度後の累積分配 = $240,000 + $90,000 = $330,000。累積利益 = $300,000。本年度分配のうち累積分配を$300,000以下に保つ部分が営業活動：$300,000 - $240,000 = $60,000営業。超過分$330,000 - $300,000 = $30,000は投資活動。答え：$60,000営業、$30,000投資。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Insurance Proceeds for Business Interruption
questions.stem: Newport Co. received $400,000 of insurance proceeds under a business interruption policy to compensate for lost profits while operations were suspended. Under ASU 2016-15, how should these proceeds be classified on the statement of cash flows?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21B, insurance proceeds are classified based on the nature of the loss. Business interruption insurance compensates for lost revenue and operating profits, so the proceeds relate to operations and are classified as operating activities. This contrasts with proceeds for the destruction of a long-lived asset, which are investing. The $400,000 is an operating inflow.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21Bにより、保険金は損失の性質に基づき分類する。事業中断保険は逸失収益と営業利益を補償するため、保険金は営業に関連し営業活動に分類する。これは長期性資産の滅失に対する保険金が投資活動となるのとは対照的である。$400,000は営業活動の流入である。

questions.subtopic: Reconciliation - Required When Using Direct Method
questions.stem: When an entity elects to use the direct method to present operating cash flows, what additional disclosure does ASC 230 require?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A reconciliation of net income to net cash provided by operating activities (the indirect method reconciliation)

choices.label: B
choices.text: A schedule of all financing activities only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Nothing additional; the direct method is complete on its own

choices.label: D
choices.text: A forecast of next year's operating cash flows

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-30 and 45-32, an entity that uses the direct method must also provide, in a separate schedule, a reconciliation of net income to net cash flow from operating activities (i.e., the indirect method presentation). This gives users the relationship between accrual-basis net income and operating cash flows. Entities using the indirect method satisfy this requirement directly in the statement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-30および45-32により、直接法を使用する事業体は、別個のスケジュールで純利益から営業活動による正味キャッシュ・フローへの調整（すなわち間接法による表示）も提供しなければならない。これにより利用者は発生主義の純利益と営業CFの関係を把握できる。間接法を使用する事業体はこの要件を計算書本体で満たす。

questions.subtopic: Operating - Working Capital with Income Taxes Payable
questions.stem: Easton Corp. reported net income of $360,000. Depreciation was $42,000. Changes: accounts receivable decreased $19,000; inventory increased $27,000; accounts payable decreased $11,000; income taxes payable increased $14,000; interest payable decreased $5,000. What is cash provided by operating activities under the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Net income $360,000 + depreciation $42,000 + decrease in AR $19,000 - increase in inventory $27,000 - decrease in AP $11,000 + increase in income taxes payable $14,000 - decrease in interest payable $5,000 = $392,000. Current asset decreases add, increases subtract; current liability increases add, decreases subtract. Computation: 360 + 42 + 19 - 27 - 11 + 14 - 5 = 392 (in thousands).
questions.explanationJa: 純利益$360,000 + 減価償却費$42,000 + 売掛金減少$19,000 - 棚卸資産増加$27,000 - 買掛金減少$11,000 + 未払法人税増加$14,000 - 未払利息減少$5,000 = $392,000。流動資産の減少は加算、増加は減算。流動負債の増加は加算、減少は減算。計算：360 + 42 + 19 - 27 - 11 + 14 - 5 = 392（千ドル）。

questions.stem: Foster Corp. paid $120,000 of interest during the year, of which $35,000 was capitalized as part of a self-constructed asset and $85,000 was expensed. For the supplemental disclosure of cash paid for interest under ASC 230, what amount is disclosed, and how is the capitalized portion classified in the body of the statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Disclose $85,000 of interest paid (total $120,000 paid less the $35,000 capitalized); the $35,000 capitalized portion is classified as an investing outflow in the body

choices.label: B
choices.text: Disclose $85,000; the capitalized portion is not disclosed and is operating

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disclose $120,000; the capitalized portion is financing in the body

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose $35,000; the expensed portion is investing in the body

questions.explanation: ASC 230-10-50-2 requires disclosure of the amount of interest paid net of amounts capitalized. Of the $120,000 of interest paid in cash, $35,000 was capitalized into the cost of the self-constructed asset, so the supplemental disclosure of cash paid for interest is $120,000 - $35,000 = $85,000. The $35,000 capitalized portion is part of the constructed asset's cost and is reported as an investing outflow in the body of the statement under ASC 230-10-45-13.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-50-2は、資産計上額を控除した支払利息額の開示を求める。現金で支払った利息$120,000のうち$35,000は自己建設資産の原価に資産化されたため、利息の現金支払の補足開示額は$120,000 - $35,000 = $85,000となる。$35,000の資産化部分は建設資産の原価の一部であり、ASC 230-10-45-13により計算書本体で投資活動の流出として報告される。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Cash Receipt from Sale of Donated Asset (NFP context excluded)
questions.stem: Granger Inc., a for-profit manufacturer, received $75,000 cash from selling a parcel of land it no longer needed for operations. The land had a carrying value of $50,000. How is the $75,000 classified on the statement of cash flows?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-12, proceeds from the sale of property, plant, and equipment (including land) are classified as investing inflows at the full cash amount received. The entire $75,000 is an investing inflow. The $25,000 gain ($75,000 - $50,000) is removed from net income in the operating section under the indirect method to avoid double counting—it is not separately reported as cash.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-12により、有形固定資産（土地を含む）の売却収入は受取現金全額で投資活動の流入に分類する。$75,000全額が投資活動の流入である。$25,000の利益（$75,000 - $50,000）は二重計上を避けるため間接法で営業活動の純利益から除外され、現金として別途報告されるものではない。

questions.subtopic: Financing - Net Cash with Capital Contributions and Distributions
questions.stem: Maddox LLC had the following during the year: members contributed $300,000 cash; the LLC distributed $120,000 cash to members; it drew $250,000 on a line of credit and repaid $180,000 of it; it paid $25,000 of debt issuance costs on a new term loan and received $500,000 of term loan proceeds. What is net cash provided by financing activities?
questions.explanation: Member contributions: +$300,000. Distributions to members: -$120,000. Line of credit draw: +$250,000. Line of credit repayment: -$180,000. Term loan proceeds: +$500,000. Debt issuance costs: -$25,000. Net financing = $300,000 - $120,000 + $250,000 - $180,000 + $500,000 - $25,000 = $725,000. All items are financing activities; debt issuance costs are financing outflows under ASC 230.

questions.stem: Pemberton Inc. holds equity securities measured at fair value through net income under ASC 321. During the year it recognized a $40,000 unrealized holding gain in net income. Net income was $520,000 and depreciation was $65,000. No securities were sold and there were no other adjustments. What is cash provided by operating activities under the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Net income $520,000 + depreciation $65,000 - unrealized holding gain $40,000 = $545,000. Under ASC 321, equity securities are marked to fair value through net income. The $40,000 unrealized gain increased net income but produced no cash (no securities were sold), so it is subtracted as a non-cash item under the indirect method. Depreciation is added back. Operating cash flow = $545,000.
questions.explanationJa: 純利益$520,000 + 減価償却費$65,000 - 未実現保有利益$40,000 = $545,000。ASC 321では持分証券は純損益を通じて公正価値で評価される。$40,000の未実現利益は純利益を増加させたが現金は生じていない（証券は売却されていない）ため、間接法では非現金項目として減算する。減価償却費は加算する。営業CF = $545,000。

questions.subtopic: Classification - Cash Paid to Settle Asset Retirement Obligation
questions.stem: Solstice Corp. paid $220,000 in cash to settle an asset retirement obligation (ARO) related to decommissioning a manufacturing facility. How is this cash payment classified on the statement of cash flows under U.S. GAAP?
questions.explanation: Cash payments to settle an asset retirement obligation are classified as operating activities. The settlement of an ARO represents the satisfaction of an accrued operating liability (the obligation was expensed over time through accretion and depreciation of the capitalized retirement cost). Because the payment relates to costs that entered the determination of net income, it is an operating outflow under ASC 230-10-45-17.
questions.explanationJa: 資産除去債務（ARO）の決済のための現金支払は営業活動に分類される。AROの決済は発生した営業負債の履行を表す（債務はアクリーションおよび資産化された除去費用の減価償却を通じて時間をかけて費用計上された）。支払は純利益の決定に含まれた費用に関連するため、ASC 230-10-45-17に基づき営業活動の流出である。

questions.subtopic: Cash Equivalents - Maturity Measured from Acquisition Date
questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, Calloway Corp. purchased a U.S. Treasury note that matures on March 1, Year 2 (five months after purchase). At December 31, Year 1, the note has two months remaining until maturity. How should the Treasury note be reported at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a cash equivalent, because the remaining maturity at the balance sheet date is three months or less

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a short-term investment, because the maturity was more than three months from the date of acquisition

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a cash equivalent, provided management intends to hold the note to maturity

choices.label: D
choices.text: As restricted cash, presented separately from cash and cash equivalents

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230, cash equivalents are short-term, highly liquid investments with original maturities of three months or less, measured from the date the investment is acquired by the entity—not from the balance sheet date. Because the note had five months to maturity when Calloway purchased it, it can never qualify as a cash equivalent, even when only two months remain at year-end. Classifying based on the remaining maturity at the balance sheet date misapplies the definition. Management's intent to hold the note does not change the maturity test. The note is not restricted cash because its use is not legally or contractually limited.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230において、現金同等物は「取得日から」満期まで3か月以内の短期・高流動性投資と定義される（貸借対照表日からの残存期間ではない）。本問の財務省証券は購入時点で満期まで5か月あったため、期末時点で残存2か月であっても現金同等物には該当せず、短期投資として報告する。経営者の保有意図は満期テストに影響しない。また使用制限があるわけではないので制限付き現金でもない。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Comprehensive Operating Cash Flow
questions.stem: Bristow Corp. reported net income of $480,000 for Year 1. The following additional information is available: depreciation expense $55,000; gain on sale of equipment $20,000; increase in accounts receivable $30,000; decrease in inventory $12,000; increase in accounts payable $25,000. Under the indirect method, what amount should Bristow report as net cash provided by operating activities?
questions.explanation: Net cash provided by operating activities = $480,000 net income + $55,000 depreciation (noncash) − $20,000 gain on sale (reported in investing) − $30,000 AR increase + $12,000 inventory decrease + $25,000 AP increase = $522,000. An answer of $562,000 results from adding the gain instead of deducting it. An answer of $508,000 results from reversing the signs of all three working capital changes. An answer of $542,000 results from omitting the gain adjustment entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 間接法による営業CF = 純利益$480,000 + 減価償却費$55,000（非資金費用の加算）− 設備売却益$20,000（投資活動に属するため控除）− 売掛金増加$30,000 + 棚卸資産減少$12,000 + 買掛金増加$25,000 = $522,000（ASC 230-10-45-28）。売却益を加算すると$562,000、運転資本3項目の符号を逆にすると$508,000、売却益の調整を忘れると$542,000となり、いずれも誤り。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Collected with Write-Offs
questions.stem: Danvers Co. reported sales revenue of $2,400,000 for the year. Accounts receivable was $310,000 at the beginning of the year and $265,000 at the end of the year. During the year, Danvers wrote off $20,000 of uncollectible accounts against the allowance. Under the direct method, what amount of cash was collected from customers?
questions.explanation: Cash collected = sales + beginning AR − ending AR − write-offs = $2,400,000 + $310,000 − $265,000 − $20,000 = $2,425,000. Write-offs reduce accounts receivable without generating cash, so $20,000 of the $45,000 decline in AR did not produce collections. An answer of $2,445,000 ignores the write-offs. An answer of $2,355,000 subtracts the AR decrease instead of adding it. An answer of $2,375,000 combines both errors.
questions.explanationJa: 顧客からの現金回収額 = 売上高 + 期首売掛金 − 期末売掛金 − 貸倒償却額 = $2,400,000 + $310,000 − $265,000 − $20,000 = $2,425,000。貸倒償却$20,000は現金を伴わずに売掛金を減少させるため、売掛金減少$45,000のうち償却分は回収額に含めない（ASC 230-10-45-25）。償却を無視すると$2,445,000、売掛金減少を減算すると$2,355,000となり誤り。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Paid to Suppliers
questions.stem: Elmhurst Inc. reported cost of goods sold of $1,150,000 for the year. During the year, inventory increased by $40,000 and accounts payable decreased by $25,000. Under the direct method, what amount of cash was paid to suppliers?
questions.explanation: Purchases = COGS + inventory increase = $1,150,000 + $40,000 = $1,190,000. Cash paid = purchases + AP decrease = $1,190,000 + $25,000 = $1,215,000. An inventory increase means more goods were bought than sold, and an AP decrease means the company paid more cash than it purchased on account—both increase cash outflows. An answer of $1,085,000 subtracts both adjustments. An answer of $1,165,000 subtracts the AP decrease. An answer of $1,175,000 ignores the inventory change.
questions.explanationJa: 仕入額 = 売上原価$1,150,000 + 棚卸資産増加$40,000 = $1,190,000。仕入先への現金支払額 = 仕入額 + 買掛金減少$25,000 = $1,215,000（ASC 230-10-45-25）。棚卸資産の増加は販売量以上の購入を、買掛金の減少は掛仕入以上の支払を意味し、いずれも現金支出を増加させる。両方を減算すると$1,085,000、買掛金減少を減算すると$1,165,000となり誤り。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Paid for Interest with Discount Amortization
questions.stem: Garfield Corp. reported interest expense of $96,000 for the year. During the year, interest payable increased by $11,000, and amortization of bond discount included in interest expense was $7,000. What amount should Garfield disclose as cash paid for interest?
questions.explanation: Cash paid for interest = interest expense − increase in interest payable − discount amortization = $96,000 − $11,000 − $7,000 = $78,000. The increase in interest payable represents expense accrued but not yet paid, and discount amortization is a noncash component of interest expense; both reduce the cash outflow below expense. An answer of $92,000 adds back the discount amortization incorrectly ($96,000 − $11,000 + $7,000). An answer of $100,000 adds the payable increase ($96,000 + $11,000 − $7,000). An answer of $114,000 adds both adjustments.
questions.explanationJa: 支払利息の現金支出額 = 利息費用$96,000 − 未払利息増加$11,000 − 社債割引発行差金償却$7,000 = $78,000。未払利息の増加は費用計上済みだが未払いの部分、割引差金償却は非資金費用であり、いずれも現金支出を費用より小さくする（ASC 230-10-50-2の補足開示）。差金償却を加算すると$92,000、未払利息増加を加算すると$100,000となり誤り。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Paid for Income Taxes with Deferred Taxes
questions.stem: Hollis Inc. reported income tax expense of $240,000 for the year. During the year, income taxes payable increased by $18,000 and the deferred tax liability increased by $32,000. What amount should Hollis disclose as cash paid for income taxes?
questions.explanation: Cash paid for income taxes = tax expense − increase in income taxes payable − increase in deferred tax liability = $240,000 − $18,000 − $32,000 = $190,000. The payable increase reflects current tax accrued but unpaid, and the deferred tax liability increase is the noncash deferred portion of tax expense. An answer of $254,000 adds the deferred tax change instead of subtracting it. An answer of $226,000 adds the payable increase instead of subtracting it. An answer of $240,000 ignores both adjustments.
questions.explanationJa: 法人税の現金支払額 = 法人税費用$240,000 − 未払法人税増加$18,000 − 繰延税金負債増加$32,000 = $190,000。未払法人税の増加は計上済み未払分、繰延税金負債の増加は税金費用のうち非資金の繰延部分であり、いずれも控除する（ASC 230-10-50-2）。繰延税金を加算すると$254,000、未払税金を加算すると$226,000となり誤り。

questions.subtopic: Loans Held for Sale - Operating Classification
questions.stem: Ironwood Mortgage Co. originates residential mortgage loans with the intent to sell them in the secondary market within 60 days. During the year, Ironwood disbursed $30,000,000 to originate such loans and received $28,500,000 from sales of loans. How should these cash flows be classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both as investing activities, because loans are financial assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: Originations as investing outflows and sale proceeds as operating inflows

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both as operating activities, because the loans are acquired specifically for resale

choices.label: D
choices.text: Originations as financing outflows and sale proceeds as financing inflows

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230, cash flows from purchases, originations, and sales of loans that are acquired specifically for resale and carried at fair value or the lower of cost or fair value are classified as operating activities, because such loans are similar to inventory held for sale in the ordinary course of business. Investing classification applies only to loans held for investment (held for the longer term to collect contractual cash flows). Financing classification is unrelated because no capital or borrowing transaction of Ironwood itself is involved.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21により、転売目的で取得・組成され、公正価値または低価法で測定されるローンの組成・購入・売却によるキャッシュフローは営業活動に分類される。これらのローンは通常の営業過程で販売される棚卸資産に類似するためである。投資活動分類は長期保有目的（投資目的）のローンにのみ適用される。自社の資本・借入取引ではないため財務活動にも該当しない。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Bond Discount Amortization Adjustment
questions.stem: Jasper Corp. issued bonds at a discount several years ago. During the current year, Jasper recorded $14,000 of bond discount amortization, which is included in interest expense. Under the indirect method, how does the discount amortization affect the reconciliation of net income to net cash provided by operating activities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is added to net income, because it is a noncash component of interest expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is deducted from net income, because it reduces the carrying amount of the bonds

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment is required, because interest is an operating item

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is reported as a financing outflow of $14,000

questions.explanation: Discount amortization increases interest expense (reducing net income) without any cash payment—cash interest paid equals only the stated coupon. Under the indirect method, the $14,000 noncash expense is therefore added back to net income. Deducting it would double the effect of the noncash charge. Making no adjustment misstates operating cash flow because net income includes an expense that consumed no cash. Discount amortization itself involves no cash, so it cannot be a financing outflow; it actually increases (not reduces) the bonds' carrying amount toward face value.
questions.explanationJa: 割引発行差金の償却は現金支出を伴わずに利息費用を増加（純利益を減少）させる。実際の現金支払はクーポン利息のみであるため、間接法では非資金費用$14,000を純利益に加算する（ASC 230-10-45-28）。控除すると非資金費用の影響を二重計上することになる。また差金償却は社債の帳簿価額を額面に向けて増加させるものであり、現金が動かない以上、財務活動の支出にも該当しない。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Deferred Tax Asset Increase
questions.stem: During the year, Kendrick Co.'s deferred tax asset increased by $26,000 because of temporary differences that will produce future tax deductions. Under the indirect method, how does this change affect the reconciliation of net income to net cash provided by operating activities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is added to net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: It has no effect on the reconciliation

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is reported as an investing inflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is deducted from net income

questions.explanation: An increase in a deferred tax asset reduces income tax expense (through a deferred tax benefit) and thereby increases net income without any corresponding cash inflow. Under the indirect method, the $26,000 increase must be deducted from net income to arrive at operating cash flow. Adding it would compound the noncash benefit. Ignoring it would overstate operating cash flow. Deferred taxes relate to operations, not investing, so no investing classification applies.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金資産の増加は繰延税金ベネフィットを通じて法人税費用を減少させ、現金収入を伴わずに純利益を増加させる。したがって間接法では増加額$26,000を純利益から控除する（ASC 230-10-45-28）。加算すると非資金の利益を二重に反映し、無調整では営業CFが過大表示となる。繰延税金は営業活動に関連するため投資活動には分類されない。

questions.subtopic: Sale of Building - Proceeds and Gain Treatment
questions.stem: Lakewood Corp. sold a building that had an original cost of $900,000 and accumulated depreciation of $620,000. The sale resulted in a gain of $45,000. Lakewood uses the indirect method. How should this transaction be reflected in the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $280,000 investing inflow; $45,000 added to net income in operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: $325,000 investing inflow; $45,000 deducted from net income in operating activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: $325,000 investing inflow; $45,000 added to net income in operating activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: $280,000 investing inflow; no adjustment in operating activities

questions.explanation: Book value = $900,000 − $620,000 = $280,000. Because the gain was $45,000, sale proceeds = $280,000 + $45,000 = $325,000, reported in full as an investing inflow. Under the indirect method, the $45,000 gain included in net income must be deducted in operating activities to avoid double counting the cash already captured in investing. Reporting only $280,000 in investing omits the gain portion of the proceeds. Adding the gain to net income would double count it.
questions.explanationJa: 帳簿価額 = $900,000 − $620,000 = $280,000。売却益$45,000より、売却収入 = $280,000 + $45,000 = $325,000であり、全額を投資活動の収入として表示する。間接法では純利益に含まれる売却益$45,000を営業活動で控除し、投資活動に計上済みの現金の二重計上を防ぐ（ASC 230-10-45-12、45-28）。投資活動に$280,000のみ計上すると収入の一部が漏れ、売却益を加算すると二重計上となる。

questions.subtopic: Shares Withheld for Employee Taxes - Financing
questions.stem: When restricted stock units vested, Marlowe Corp. withheld shares from employees to satisfy the employees' statutory income tax withholding obligation and remitted $180,000 cash to the taxing authority on the employees' behalf. Under ASU 2016-09 amendments to ASC 230, how should the $180,000 payment be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activity, because it relates to payroll taxes

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activity, because shares were acquired

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activity, because it is in substance a repurchase of the entity's equity instruments

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-09 requires cash paid to a taxing authority when an employer withholds shares to satisfy employees' statutory tax withholding to be classified as a financing activity. Economically, the employer has repurchased a portion of the vested shares from employees, which is a treasury-stock-like transaction. It is not an ordinary payroll-related operating payment because it substitutes for delivering shares. It is not investing because the entity is dealing in its own equity. Cash was actually remitted, so it is not a noncash transaction.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09（ASC 230-10-45-15）により、従業員の源泉税納付目的で株式を源泉留保し、雇用主が税務当局へ現金を納付した場合、その支払は財務活動に分類される。実質的に従業員から自社株式の一部を買い戻す自己株式取得類似の取引であるためである。通常の給与関連支出（営業活動）ではなく、自社株式に関する取引なので投資活動でもない。現金が実際に支払われているため非資金取引でもない。

questions.stem: Norwell Inc. presents the following balances. Beginning of year: cash and cash equivalents $150,000; restricted cash $40,000. End of year: cash and cash equivalents $210,000; restricted cash $25,000. Under ASU 2016-18, what net change should the statement of cash flows reconcile?
questions.explanation: ASU 2016-18 requires the statement of cash flows to explain the change in the total of cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash. Beginning total = $150,000 + $40,000 = $190,000; ending total = $210,000 + $25,000 = $235,000; net change = $45,000 increase. An answer of $60,000 considers only unrestricted cash. An answer of $15,000 decrease considers only restricted cash. An answer of $75,000 incorrectly adds the two separate changes as absolute values.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-18（ASC 230-10-45-4）により、キャッシュフロー計算書は現金・現金同等物・制限付き現金の「合計」の増減を説明する。期首合計 = $150,000 + $40,000 = $190,000、期末合計 = $210,000 + $25,000 = $235,000、純増加 = $45,000。非制限現金のみを見ると$60,000、制限付き現金のみだと$15,000減少となり、いずれも誤り。

questions.subtopic: Transfers Between Restricted and Unrestricted Cash
questions.stem: During the year, Oakhurst Co. transferred $500,000 from its operating cash account into an escrow account (restricted cash) as required by a debt covenant. No other party received the funds. Under ASU 2016-18, how is this transfer presented in the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a $500,000 financing outflow, because the escrow relates to debt

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is not presented as a cash flow, because restricted cash is included in the total reconciled on the statement

questions.explanation: Because ASU 2016-18 requires restricted cash to be combined with cash and cash equivalents in the beginning and ending totals on the statement of cash flows, internal transfers between unrestricted cash and restricted cash do not change the combined total and therefore are not reported as operating, investing, or financing cash flows. Presenting the transfer in any activity category would misstate cash flows, since no cash entered or left the combined pool.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-18により制限付き現金はキャッシュフロー計算書の期首・期末合計に含めて調整されるため、非制限現金と制限付き現金の間の内部振替は合計額を変化させず、営業・投資・財務いずれの活動にもキャッシュフローとして表示しない。現金が合算プールの外に出入りしていない以上、いずれかの区分に表示すればキャッシュフローの過大表示となる。

questions.stem: During Year 1, holders of Pinecrest Corp.'s convertible preferred stock converted preferred shares with a carrying amount of $1,500,000 into 100,000 shares of common stock. No cash was exchanged. How should Pinecrest report this conversion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a financing inflow and financing outflow of $1,500,000 each

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a noncash financing activity disclosed in a supplemental schedule or note

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a financing outflow of $1,500,000 only

choices.label: D
choices.text: No reporting or disclosure is required, because total equity is unchanged

questions.explanation: The conversion of preferred stock into common stock is a noncash financing transaction: it changes the entity's capital structure without any cash receipt or payment. ASC 230 requires noncash investing and financing activities to be disclosed in a separate schedule or in the notes, not in the body of the statement of cash flows. Reporting offsetting financing inflows and outflows would fabricate cash flows that never occurred. Disclosure is required even though total equity is unchanged, because the information is relevant to understanding financing activities.
questions.explanationJa: 優先株式から普通株式への転換は、現金の授受なしに資本構成を変化させる非資金財務取引である。ASC 230-10-50-3〜6により、非資金の投資・財務活動はキャッシュフロー計算書本体ではなく別表または注記で開示する。発生していない現金流入・流出を相殺表示するのは誤りであり、純資産合計が不変であっても財務活動の理解に有用なため開示は必要である。

questions.subtopic: Insurance Settlement Allocated by Nature of Loss
questions.stem: Quincy Corp.'s warehouse fire destroyed both inventory and the building. Quincy received a single lump-sum insurance settlement of $900,000, of which $350,000 was identified as compensation for the destroyed inventory and $550,000 for the destroyed building. Under ASU 2016-15, how should the proceeds be classified?

choices.label: C
choices.text: $350,000 as an operating inflow and $550,000 as an investing inflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: $550,000 as an operating inflow and $350,000 as an investing inflow

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-15 requires insurance settlement proceeds to be classified on the basis of the nature of the related loss, and a lump-sum settlement must be allocated among categories based on the nature of each loss covered. Inventory is an operating asset, so the $350,000 recovery is an operating inflow; the building is a productive asset, so the $550,000 recovery is an investing inflow. Classifying the entire amount in a single category ignores the required allocation, and reversing the amounts mismatches each recovery with the nature of its loss.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-15（ASC 230-10-45-21B）により、保険金収入は補償対象となった損失の性質に基づいて分類し、一括受領額は損失の性質ごとに配分する。棚卸資産は営業資産であるため$350,000は営業活動の収入、建物は生産的資産であるため$550,000は投資活動の収入となる。全額を単一区分に分類したり金額を逆に配分するのは誤り。

questions.subtopic: Contingent Consideration Paid Soon After Acquisition
questions.stem: Redfern Inc. acquired a business on March 1, Year 1. The purchase agreement required a contingent payment that Redfern settled for $700,000 cash on April 15, Year 1—approximately six weeks after the acquisition date. Under ASU 2016-15, how should the $700,000 payment be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Investing activity, because it was made soon after the business combination

choices.label: B
choices.text: Financing activity up to the acquisition-date fair value of the liability

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-15, contingent consideration payments made soon after the acquisition date (generally within a relatively short period such as three months or less) are classified as investing activities, as part of the cash paid to acquire the business. The split between financing (up to the acquisition-date fair value of the liability) and operating (any excess) applies only to payments made beyond that initial period. The payment involves actual cash, so noncash disclosure treatment does not apply.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-15により、企業結合の取得日から短期間内（おおむね3か月以内）に支払われた条件付対価は、事業取得のための現金支出の一部として投資活動に分類される。取得日公正価値までを財務活動、超過分を営業活動とする区分は、取得日から相当期間経過後の支払にのみ適用される。実際に現金が支払われているため非資金取引の開示扱いにはならない。

questions.stem: Sandpiper Corp. securitized trade receivables and received, in addition to cash, a beneficial interest in the transferred receivables. In the following year, Sandpiper collected $260,000 of cash payments on that beneficial interest. Under ASU 2016-15, how should the $260,000 of collections be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activity, because the underlying assets were trade receivables

choices.label: B
choices.text: Financing activity, because securitization is a form of borrowing

choices.label: C
choices.text: Split between operating and financing based on the interest's fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-15, cash receipts from payments on a transferor's beneficial interest in securitized trade receivables are classified as investing activities. The beneficial interest is an investment received in the securitization (its initial receipt is disclosed as a noncash activity), so subsequent collections on it are returns of/on an investment, not operating collections of trade receivables. The transfer qualified as a sale, not a borrowing, so financing classification is inapplicable, and no fair-value-based split is prescribed.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-15（ASC 230-10-45-12）により、譲渡人が売上債権の証券化で受け取った受益権に対するその後の現金回収は投資活動に分類される。受益権は証券化で受領した投資（当初受領は非資金取引として開示）であり、その回収は投資のリターンであって売上債権の営業上の回収ではない。譲渡は売却処理されており借入ではないため財務活動には該当せず、公正価値による按分も規定されていない。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Multiple Working Capital Changes
questions.stem: Talbot Co. reported net income of $250,000 and depreciation expense of $31,000. Balance sheet changes during the year: accounts receivable decreased $22,000; inventory increased $35,000; prepaid expenses increased $8,000; accounts payable decreased $14,000; accrued liabilities increased $9,000. Under the indirect method, what is net cash provided by operating activities?
questions.explanation: Operating cash flow = $250,000 + $31,000 depreciation + $22,000 AR decrease − $35,000 inventory increase − $8,000 prepaid increase − $14,000 AP decrease + $9,000 accrued liabilities increase = $255,000. An answer of $325,000 results from adding the $35,000 inventory increase instead of subtracting it (a $70,000 error). An answer of $224,000 omits the depreciation add-back. An answer of $239,000 reverses the signs of the AR and AP changes.
questions.explanationJa: 営業CF = 純利益$250,000 + 減価償却費$31,000 + 売掛金減少$22,000 − 棚卸資産増加$35,000 − 前払費用増加$8,000 − 買掛金減少$14,000 + 未払費用増加$9,000 = $255,000（ASC 230-10-45-28）。棚卸資産増加を加算すると$325,000、減価償却費の加算を忘れると$224,000、売掛金と買掛金の符号を逆にすると$239,000となり誤り。

questions.subtopic: Accumulated Depreciation Rollforward - Deriving Depreciation Expense
questions.stem: Updike Corp.'s accumulated depreciation was $800,000 at the beginning of the year and $920,000 at the end of the year. During the year, Updike sold equipment with accumulated depreciation of $150,000. There were no other disposals or impairments. What amount of depreciation expense should be added back to net income under the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Ending accumulated depreciation = beginning + depreciation expense − accumulated depreciation removed on disposals. Solving: $920,000 = $800,000 + expense − $150,000, so depreciation expense = $270,000. An answer of $120,000 is merely the net change in the account and ignores the disposal. An answer of $150,000 is the accumulated depreciation removed, not the expense. An answer of $420,000 double counts the disposal by adding it twice.
questions.explanationJa: 期末減価償却累計額 = 期首 + 当期減価償却費 − 売却資産の累計額除去分。$920,000 = $800,000 + 減価償却費 − $150,000 より、減価償却費 = $270,000。これが間接法で純利益に加算される金額となる（ASC 230-10-45-28）。勘定の純増減$120,000は処分の影響を無視しており、$150,000は除去された累計額にすぎず、$420,000は処分額を二重に加えた誤り。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Vintner Corp. had the following cash transactions: issued common stock for $400,000; paid cash dividends of $120,000; repaid bonds payable of $250,000; purchased treasury stock for $80,000; received proceeds of $60,000 from a short-term bank loan. What amount should Vintner report as net cash provided by (used in) financing activities?
questions.explanation: Net financing cash flow = $400,000 stock issuance − $120,000 dividends − $250,000 bond repayment − $80,000 treasury stock + $60,000 short-term borrowing = $10,000 provided. All five items are financing activities; short-term bank borrowings are financing regardless of maturity. An answer of $130,000 omits the dividend payment. An answer of $(50,000) omits the short-term borrowing. An answer of $170,000 treats the treasury stock purchase as an inflow rather than an outflow.
questions.explanationJa: 財務活動による純CF = 普通株式発行$400,000 − 配当支払$120,000 − 社債償還$250,000 − 自己株式取得$80,000 + 短期借入$60,000 = $10,000の純収入（ASC 230-10-45-14、45-15）。短期借入も満期にかかわらず財務活動である。配当支払を漏らすと$130,000、短期借入を漏らすと$(50,000)、自己株式取得を収入として扱うと$170,000となり誤り。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Westbrook Inc. had the following cash transactions: purchased equipment for $275,000; received $90,000 from the sale of land; purchased available-for-sale debt securities for $130,000; collected $50,000 of principal on a long-term note receivable; received $12,000 of interest on the note. What amount should Westbrook report as net cash used in investing activities?
questions.explanation: Net investing = −$275,000 equipment + $90,000 land proceeds − $130,000 AFS securities + $50,000 principal collection = $(265,000) used. Interest received of $12,000 is an operating inflow under U.S. GAAP and is excluded. An answer of $253,000 incorrectly includes the interest in investing. An answer of $315,000 omits the principal collection. An answer of $185,000 includes only the equipment and land transactions.
questions.explanationJa: 投資活動による純CF = 設備購入−$275,000 + 土地売却$90,000 − AFS債券購入−$130,000 + 貸付金元本回収$50,000 = 純支出$265,000（ASC 230-10-45-12、45-13）。利息受取$12,000は米国GAAPでは営業活動の収入であり除外する。利息を含めると$253,000、元本回収を漏らすと$315,000、設備と土地のみだと$185,000となり誤り。

questions.stem: Under ASC 230, what is the primary purpose of the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To report the financial position of the entity at a point in time

choices.label: B
choices.text: To provide relevant information about an entity's cash receipts and cash payments during a period

choices.label: C
choices.text: To reconcile retained earnings to net income for the period

choices.label: D
choices.text: To present the entity's profitability on the accrual basis of accounting

questions.explanation: ASC 230-10-10-1 states that the primary purpose of the statement of cash flows is to provide relevant information about the cash receipts and cash payments of an entity during a period, helping users assess liquidity, solvency, and the ability to generate future cash flows. Financial position at a point in time is the purpose of the balance sheet. The statement of stockholders' equity reconciles retained earnings. Accrual-basis profitability is presented in the income statement, not the cash flow statement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-10-1により、キャッシュフロー計算書の主目的は、一会計期間における企業の現金収入と現金支出に関する有用な情報を提供することであり、流動性・支払能力・将来キャッシュフロー創出能力の評価に役立てる。一時点の財政状態は貸借対照表、利益剰余金の調整は株主資本等変動計算書、発生主義による収益性は損益計算書の役割である。

questions.stem: Yarrow Corp. disposed of a component that qualifies as a discontinued operation. How are the cash flows of the discontinued operation presented in Yarrow's statement of cash flows under U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a separate fourth category following financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Excluded from the statement of cash flows entirely

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included within operating, investing, and financing activities, with either total operating and investing cash flows of the discontinued operation or specified items disclosed

choices.label: D
choices.text: Netted into a single line within operating activities

questions.explanation: Cash flows of a discontinued operation are not presented as a separate category; they remain within the three standard classifications. ASC 205-20 requires disclosure of either the total operating and investing cash flows of the discontinued operation or its depreciation, amortization, capital expenditures, and significant noncash items. The cash flows are real flows of the entity and cannot be excluded. Compressing all of them into one operating line would misclassify investing and financing flows of the component.
questions.explanationJa: 非継続事業のキャッシュフローは独立した第4区分としては表示せず、営業・投資・財務の3区分の中に含めて表示する。ASC 205-20-50-5B(c)により、非継続事業の営業・投資キャッシュフロー合計、または減価償却費・設備投資・重要な非資金項目等のいずれかを開示する。企業の実際のキャッシュフローであるため除外はできず、全額を営業活動の1行に圧縮すると投資・財務のフローが誤区分となる。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Unrealized Foreign Currency Transaction Loss
questions.stem: Zephyr Co. has a euro-denominated account payable that remains unsettled at year-end. Remeasurement of the payable at the year-end exchange rate produced a $16,000 foreign currency transaction loss, which is included in net income. Under the indirect method, how does this loss affect the operating activities section?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is added back to net income, because it is an unrealized noncash loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is deducted from net income, because the payable increased

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is reported in the separate line for the effect of exchange rate changes on cash

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment is needed, because the loss relates to an operating liability

questions.explanation: The remeasurement loss reduced net income but involved no cash payment because the payable is still outstanding. Under the indirect method, the $16,000 unrealized loss is added back to net income. Deducting it would double its effect. The separate exchange-rate line on the statement applies only to the effect of rate changes on cash balances themselves, not on payables. Making no adjustment would understate operating cash flow by the amount of a noncash charge.
questions.explanationJa: 未決済の外貨建買掛金の期末換算（再測定）による為替差損$16,000は純利益を減少させたが、現金支出を伴わない未実現損失である。したがって間接法では純利益に加算する（ASC 230-10-45-28、ASC 830-20）。控除すると影響が二重になる。計算書末尾の「為替レート変動の現金への影響」は現金残高自体への影響のみを表示する行であり、買掛金の換算差損には適用されない。

questions.subtopic: Assets Rented to Others and Subsequently Sold
questions.stem: Acorn Equipment Co. manufactures machinery that it first rents to customers under short-term rental agreements and then routinely sells when the machines come off rent. During the year, Acorn received $2,000,000 of rental payments and $3,500,000 from sales of machines that had been rented. How should these receipts be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Rentals as operating inflows; sales proceeds as investing inflows

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both as investing inflows, because the machines are productive assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Rentals as investing inflows; sales proceeds as operating inflows

questions.explanation: ASC 230 classifies as operating activities the cash receipts from both rents and subsequent sales of assets that an entity routinely rents to others and then sells in the ordinary course of business. For such an entity, the machines function like inventory, so the sale proceeds are operating—not investing—inflows. Treating the sale proceeds as investing would apply only to productive assets held for use in operations rather than for rental-then-sale in the normal business cycle.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-16により、通常の営業過程で他者に賃貸した後に売却することを常とする資産については、賃貸料収入とその後の売却収入の両方を営業活動に分類する。このような企業にとって当該機械は実質的に棚卸資産として機能するため、売却収入も投資活動ではなく営業活動となる。投資活動への分類は、自社の事業で使用する目的で保有する生産的資産の売却にのみ適用される。

questions.subtopic: Direct Method - Cash Paid for Operating Expenses
questions.stem: Birchwood Co. reported total operating expenses of $620,000, which include depreciation expense of $70,000. During the year, prepaid expenses decreased by $9,000 and accrued operating liabilities increased by $13,000. Under the direct method, what amount of cash was paid for operating expenses?
questions.explanation: Cash paid = operating expenses − depreciation (noncash) − decrease in prepaid expenses − increase in accrued liabilities = $620,000 − $70,000 − $9,000 − $13,000 = $528,000. A prepaid decrease means expense was recognized from amounts paid in prior periods, and an accrual increase means expense was recognized before payment—both reduce current cash outflows. An answer of $550,000 ignores both working capital changes. An answer of $572,000 reverses the signs of the working capital changes. An answer of $598,000 fails to remove depreciation.
questions.explanationJa: 営業費用の現金支払額 = 営業費用$620,000 − 減価償却費$70,000（非資金）− 前払費用減少$9,000 − 未払費用増加$13,000 = $528,000（ASC 230-10-45-25）。前払費用の減少は過年度支払分の費用化、未払費用の増加は未払いのままの費用計上を意味し、いずれも当期の現金支出を減少させる。運転資本変動を無視すると$550,000、符号を逆にすると$572,000、減価償却費を除かないと$598,000となり誤り。

questions.subtopic: Ending Cash Balance with Exchange Rate Effect
questions.stem: Crandall Corp. reports the following for Year 1: net cash provided by operating activities $320,000; net cash used in investing activities $410,000; net cash provided by financing activities $150,000; effect of exchange rate changes on cash $12,000 (increase). Cash and cash equivalents at the beginning of the year were $95,000. What is the ending balance of cash and cash equivalents?
questions.explanation: Net change in cash = $320,000 − $410,000 + $150,000 + $12,000 = $72,000 increase. Ending cash = $95,000 + $72,000 = $167,000. The effect of exchange rate changes on cash is a required separate reconciling line and must be included in the total change. An answer of $155,000 omits the exchange rate effect. An answer of $72,000 is the net change, not the ending balance. An answer of $60,000 is the net change before the exchange rate effect.
questions.explanationJa: 現金の純増減 = $320,000 − $410,000 + $150,000 + 為替レート変動の影響$12,000 = $72,000の増加。期末現金 = 期首$95,000 + $72,000 = $167,000。為替レート変動が現金に与える影響は独立の調整行として表示し、純増減に含める（ASC 230-10-45-18）。為替影響を漏らすと$155,000、$72,000は純増減額であって期末残高ではない。

questions.subtopic: Direct vs. Indirect Method - Key Relationships
questions.stem: Which of the following statements about the direct and indirect methods of presenting operating cash flows is correct under ASC 230?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both methods report the same total net cash provided by operating activities, and only the operating section differs in presentation

choices.label: B
choices.text: The indirect method generally reports higher operating cash flow than the direct method

choices.label: C
choices.text: The FASB requires the direct method for public business entities

choices.label: D
choices.text: The investing and financing sections differ depending on which method is chosen

questions.explanation: The direct and indirect methods are alternative presentations of the same operating cash flows, so the net total of operating activities is identical under either method; only the format of the operating section differs. The FASB encourages but does not require the direct method—most entities use the indirect method. The investing and financing sections are presented identically regardless of the method chosen for operating activities.
questions.explanationJa: 直接法と間接法は同一の営業キャッシュフローの表示方法の違いにすぎず、営業活動による純額はどちらの方法でも一致する。異なるのは営業活動セクションの表示形式のみである。FASBは直接法を推奨するが強制はしておらず（ASC 230-10-45-25）、実務では間接法が大多数である。投資活動・財務活動セクションは選択した方法にかかわらず同一に表示される。

questions.stem: Which of the following statements regarding cash equivalents under ASC 230 is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Any security maturing within one year qualifies as a cash equivalent

choices.label: B
choices.text: Money market mutual funds can never be presented within cash and cash equivalents

choices.label: C
choices.text: Common stock of a highly liquid public company qualifies if management intends to sell it within three months

choices.label: D
choices.text: Equity securities are generally excluded from cash equivalents, although preferred stock acquired shortly before its specified redemption date may qualify

questions.explanation: Cash equivalents must be readily convertible to known amounts of cash and so near maturity that they present insignificant risk of value changes from interest rate movements. Equity securities have no maturity date and fluctuate in value, so they are generally excluded; however, ASC 230 cites preferred stock acquired within three months of its specified redemption date as an example that can qualify because redemption fixes the amount and timing. A one-year maturity is far too long. Money market funds commonly qualify. Intent to sell common stock does not create a maturity or a known redemption amount.
questions.explanationJa: 現金同等物は既知の金額の現金に容易に転換でき、金利変動による価値変動リスクが僅少なほど満期が近い投資でなければならない（ASC 230-10-20）。持分証券は満期がなく価値が変動するため原則として除外されるが、償還日が定められた優先株式を償還日の3か月以内前に取得した場合は金額と時期が確定しているため該当し得る、とASC 230は例示している。満期1年は長すぎ、MMFは通常該当し、普通株式の売却意図は満期や確定償還額を生まない。

questions.subtopic: Indirect Method - Discount Accretion on Bond Investment
questions.stem: Driscoll Inc. holds a held-to-maturity bond investment purchased at a discount. During the year, interest income of $42,000 was recognized, of which $6,000 represented accretion of the discount; the remainder was received in cash. Under the indirect method, how is the discount accretion treated in the operating activities section?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Added to net income as a noncash expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deducted from net income, because it is noncash interest income

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment, because all interest income is operating

questions.explanation: Discount accretion on a bond investment increases interest income and net income without any cash receipt—only $36,000 of the $42,000 was collected in cash. Under the indirect method, the $6,000 noncash income must be deducted from net income. Adding it back applies only to noncash expenses, which this is not. No cash moved, so no investing inflow exists. Failing to adjust would overstate operating cash flow by $6,000.
questions.explanationJa: 債券投資の割引差金（ディスカウント）の償却（アクリーション）は、現金受取を伴わずに利息収益と純利益を増加させる。実際の現金受取は$42,000のうち$36,000のみである。したがって間接法では非資金収益$6,000を純利益から控除する（ASC 230-10-45-28）。加算調整は非資金費用に対するものでありこれには該当しない。現金は動いていないため投資活動の収入も生じず、無調整では営業CFが$6,000過大となる。

questions.subtopic: Dividends Paid - Computed from Payable Balances
questions.stem: Ellsworth Corp. declared cash dividends of $90,000 during Year 1. Dividends payable was $15,000 at the beginning of the year and $22,000 at the end of the year. What amount should Ellsworth report as dividends paid in the financing activities section?
questions.explanation: Dividends paid = beginning dividends payable + dividends declared − ending dividends payable = $15,000 + $90,000 − $22,000 = $83,000, reported as a financing outflow. Only the cash actually disbursed appears on the statement; the declaration itself is not a cash flow. An answer of $90,000 is the amount declared, not paid. An answer of $97,000 reverses the payable balances ($90,000 + $22,000 − $15,000). An answer of $68,000 subtracts the ending payable from declarations without adding the beginning balance.
questions.explanationJa: 配当支払額 = 期首未払配当金$15,000 + 当期宣言額$90,000 − 期末未払配当金$22,000 = $83,000で、財務活動の支出として表示する（ASC 230-10-45-15）。計算書に表示されるのは実際に支払われた現金のみで、宣言自体はキャッシュフローではない。$90,000は宣言額、$97,000は期首・期末を逆にした誤り、$68,000は期首残高を加算し忘れた誤り。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, which of the following lease-related cash payments by a lessee is classified as a financing activity on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Repayment of the principal portion of the finance lease liability

choices.label: B
choices.text: Fixed payments on an operating lease

choices.label: C
choices.text: Variable lease payments not included in the lease liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: Payments on a short-term lease for which the recognition exemption was elected

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-45-5, a lessee classifies repayments of the principal portion of a finance lease liability as financing activities, while the interest portion follows ASC 230 (operating for most entities). Operating lease payments, variable lease payments not included in the measurement of the lease liability, and short-term lease payments are all classified as operating activities, because they are expensed as part of ordinary operations rather than repayments of recognized financing obligations.
